report:
NARRATIVE:
RADIOGRAPHIC EXAMINATION OF THE CHEST: 09
/02/14
 
CLINICAL HISTORY: 44 years of age, Female, Eval PTX.
 
COMPARISON: 2/14/2009
 
PROCEDURE COMMENTS: Single view of the chest. 
 
FINDINGS:
 
There is a right sided dialysis catheter and a tracheostomy catheter. 
There is a pigtail drain in the left inferior hemithorax. There is a 
left internal jugular central venous catheter.
 
There is extensive consolidation in the left lung, reflecting 
necrotizing pneumonia. The left pneumothorax and chest wall 
subcutaneous edema are again visualized. There are small faintly 
visualized nodules in the right lung. There is been an interval 
increase in left chest wall subcutaneous emphysema.
 
IMPRESSION:
 
1.  Interval increase in left chest wall subcutaneous emphysema.
 
2.  Extensive consolidation in left lung, reflective of necrotizing 
pneumonia.
 
3.  Redemonstration of left pneumothorax.
 
4.  Small, faintly visualized right lung nodules.

RADIOGRAPHIC EXAMINATION OF THE CHEST: 5/18/2018
 
CLINICAL HISTORY: 26 years of age, Male, CF patient with decreasing 
oxygen saturations on RA, dull back pain radiating to chest and SOB, 
r/o PNA, r/o PTx.
 
COMPARISON: 5/18/2018.
 
PROCEDURE COMMENTS: Two views of the chest. 
 
FINDINGS:
 
Stable appearance of a right arm PICC line. Redemonstration of 
bronchiectasis and diffuse coarse pulmonary opacities consistent with 
history of cystic fibrosis. Redemonstration of consolidative opacity 
in the left lung base, without significant interval change. No 
visible pneumothorax. Stable cardiomediastinal silhouette. No acute 
osseous abnormality.

the left base. No visible pneumothorax.

report:
NARRATIVE:
Chest, single view.
 
History: 63-year-old female with the following left thoracoscopy. 
History of complex regional pain syndrome. 
 
Comparison: Two views of the chest dated 10/24/19.
 
Findings: A single portable supine intraoperative film shows 
intubation of the left mainstem bronchus.  Lung volumes are low.  
There is continued pleural thickening along the left lateral chest 
wall without evidence of pneumothorax.  There is more conspicuous 
widening of the mediastinum, which is likely related to supine 
portable film as well as low lung volumes. There is extensive 
subcutaneous emphysema within the left neck, along the lower left 
chest wall, and within the left breast.  The imaged osseous 
structures are unremarkable.
 
IMPRESSION: 
 
1.EXTENSIVE SUBCUTANEOUS EMPHYSEMA WITHIN THE LEFT LATERAL CHEST 
WALL, LEFT NECK, AND LEFT BREAST.  NO EVIDENCE OF PNEUMOTHORAX.  
FINDINGS WERE DISCUSSED WITH Dr Wesley ON 10/24/2019 AT 1:40 
PM.
 
2.APPARENT INCREASED WIDENING OF THE MEDIASTINUM, WHICH IS LIKELY 
SECONDARY TO TECHNICAL FACTORS AS WELL AS LOW LUNG VOLUMES.  
 
SUMMARY: 4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDING, MAY NEED ACTION

report:
NARRATIVE:
CHEST TWO VIEWS:   5-24-2005 
 
 COMPARISON:   05/24
 
 CLINICAL DATA:    This is an 83 year old here to evaluate fluid 
status.
 
 FINDINGS:  
 
 Two views of the chest demonstrate postoperative changes including 
sternotomy, mediastinal clips, and ostial markers consistent with 
coronary artery bypass. There is a left chest wall dual lead AICD 
with tips overlying the right atrium and right ventricle.  There is 
persistent pulmonary edema with cardiomegaly.  There are opacities in 
the right greater than left lung bases with small bilateral 
effusions.  
 
 IMPRESSION:  
 
 EVIDENCE OF HEART FAILURE AS DESCRIBED.

report:
NARRATIVE:
SINGLE VIEW OF THE CHEST:  3-19-2017.
SINGLE VIEW OF THE CHEST:  3-19-2017 AT 1822 HOURS.
SINGLE VIEW OF THE CHEST:  3/19/2017 AT 2214 HOURS.
CLINICAL HISTORY:  Central line.
COMPARISON EXAMINATIONS:  None.
FINDINGS:  Right central line tip in superior vena cava.  No
associated pneumothorax.
Thoracic scoliosis convexed to right may be positional in nature.
Abnormal increased opacity involving inferior one-half of right
hemithorax.  Associated right basilar atelectasis.  Lung parenchyma
is otherwise clear.  Incidental note made of deformity of right
shoulder and clavicle.
Chest x-ray of 3/19/2017 at 1822 hours, demonstrates improved lung
volumes.  Abnormal opacification, inferior half of right hemithorax
persists.  Lung parenchyma is clear.
Chest x-ray 3-19-2017 at 2214 hours, single lateral view demonstrates
blunt posterior costophrenic sulci bilaterally, suggestive of
bilateral pleural effusions.
IMPRESSION:
1.  RIGHT INTERNAL JUGULAR LINE TIP IN SUPERIOR VENA CAVA.  NO
PNEUMOTHORAX.
2.  NORMAL OPACIFICATION INFERIOR RIGHT HEMITHORAX.  A COMPONENT OF
WHICH IS LIKELY PLEURAL EFFUSION.  RECOMMEND COMPARISON TO PRIOR
OUTSIDE STUDIES TO DOCUMENT EVOLUTION OF THIS FINDING.  ALTERNATELY,
A DECUBITUS FILM OR CROSS SECTIONAL IMAGING CAN BE DONE TO FURTHER
EVALUATE.
3.  SMALL LEFT-SIDED PLEURAL EFFUSION.
4.  DEFORMITY OF RIGHT SHOULDER.
SUMMARY:4-POSSIBLE SIGNIFICANT FINDINGS, MAY NEED ACTION

124 323 000 582 20
